About

I'm Christian V. Mercado, a freelance automation builder from Palo, Leyte, Philippines. I help individuals and small businesses eliminate repetitive tasks by building reliable, intelligent workflows.

My focus is on no-code and AI-powered automation — connecting tools like Make.com, Zapier, ClickUp, Slack, and Google Workspace to create systems that run themselves.

I'm passionate about making automation accessible and practical, and I'm always looking for new problems to solve with the right workflow.

📩
AI-Powered Customer Inquiry Automation System
AI-powered inquiry handling — from form submission to logged response, fully automated.
Make.comWebhooksAI classificationGmailGoogle Sheets

Screenshots

Make.com flow — webhook → AI agent → router → Gmail & Sheets
AI-generated Gmail response sent to customer automatically

Overview

This project replaces manual inquiry handling with a fully automated pipeline. Users submit a message through a simple web form — AI classifies the category and priority, sends a Gmail reply, and logs everything to Google Sheets without any human in the loop.

How it works

1
Form submission — User submits inquiry via web form.
2
Webhook trigger — Form fires a webhook instantly to Make.com.
3
AI agent classifies — Category and priority (High / Medium / Low) assigned.
4
JSON parsed → Router — Output structured and branched by priority.
5
Gmail sends response — Context-aware reply sent per priority branch.
6
Google Sheets logs everything — Full record of every inquiry and response.

Key outcomes

Zero manual sorting — every inquiry auto-categorized and prioritized
Instant AI-generated Gmail replies with no delay
Full audit trail in Google Sheets

Tools used

Make.comWebhooksMake AI AgentsJSON parserRouterGmailGoogle Sheets

No-code automation

Category

AI & ML Automation
Business process automation

Conflict-free appointment booking
Full appointment management — booking confirmation, conflict detection, cancellation & rescheduling — all automated.
Make.comGoogle SheetsGoogle CalendarGmailRescheduleCancel

Screenshots

Make.com automation flow — Sheets → Calendar conflict check → Router → Gmail
Google Sheets logging all booking form responses
Google Calendar auto-populated with confirmed appointments
Branded confirmation email — V & G Health Center
Automated cancellation email sent via Gmail
Cancel/Reschedule Make.com scenario — full updated flow

Overview

Built for V & G Health Center, this automation handles the full appointment lifecycle — booking, conflict detection, cancellation, and rescheduling. It watches Google Sheets for new requests, scans Google Calendar for conflicts, and automatically sends branded Gmail notifications for every action.

The latest update adds Cancel & Reschedule flows: patients can cancel or reschedule existing appointments and the system handles everything automatically — updating the calendar, logging to Sheets, and emailing confirmation.

How it works

1
Form responses logged — Patient submits request (book / cancel / reschedule) via Google Sheets form.
2
Make.com watches new rows — Triggers every 15 minutes on new entries.
3
Router detects intent — Checks if the request is a new booking, cancellation, or reschedule.
4a
New booking — Conflict check runs; if slot is free, Calendar event created & confirmation email sent.
4b
Cancellation — Existing Calendar event deleted, Google Sheets row updated, cancellation email sent.
4c
Reschedule — Old event deleted, conflict check on new slot, new event created, reschedule confirmation email sent.

Key outcomes

Full appointment lifecycle handled — book, cancel, reschedule — zero manual work
Eliminates double bookings via real-time Google Calendar conflict detection
Branded Gmail notifications for every action — confirmation, cancellation & reschedule
All responses and statuses logged and traceable in Google Sheets

Tools used

Make.comGoogle SheetsGoogle CalendarGmailRouterCancel logicReschedule logic

Type

No-code automation

Category

Scheduling automation
Appointment management
Business process automation

AI Operations Manager
An AI agent that reads client messages, handles multiple intents at once, creates tasks in ClickUp, alerts the team on Slack, and sends status updates via Gmail — all automatically.
Make.comAI AgentClickUpSlackGmail

Screenshots

Full Make.com scenario — AI agent parses intents and routes to ClickUp, Slack & Gmail
ClickUp tasks auto-created
Slack #clickup-task alert
AI follow-up comments
Status update — Design Book Cover
Status update — Calling Card Design

Overview

This is an AI-powered operations system that acts as a virtual manager. When a client sends a message — even one with multiple requests — the AI reads all intents, decides what action each requires, and executes them simultaneously: creating ClickUp tasks, adding follow-up comments, notifying the team on Slack, and sending Gmail status updates.

How it works

1
Client message received — Via email, form, or webhook trigger.
2
Make AI Agent parses intents — Detects all actions and returns structured JSON.
3
Router branches by intent — New task, follow-up, status check each take their own path.
4
New task → ClickUp + Slack — Task created, team notified in #clickup-task.
5
Follow-up → ClickUp comment — Timestamped comment added to existing task.
6
Status check → Gmail — Current status fetched and emailed to client.

Key outcomes

Handles multiple intents in a single message — no missed requests
ClickUp tasks created and updated automatically — zero manual entry
Team instantly notified on Slack with full client context
Clients receive real-time task status updates via Gmail
Operates like a full-time operations assistant — 24/7

Tools used

Make.comMake AI AgentsClickUpSlackGmailRouterJSON parser

Invoice automation
Tally form submission triggers automatic PDF invoice generation and delivery via Gmail — fully hands-free.
Make.comTallyGoogle SheetsPDF APIGmail

Screenshots

Make.com flow — Tally → Google Sheets → HTTP PDF generator → Gmail delivery
Auto-generated PDF invoice — client name, description, amount & payment details
Gmail delivery — invoice sent as PDF attachment with amount & due date in email body

Overview

This automation eliminates manual invoice creation entirely. When a client fills out a Tally form, Make.com instantly logs the data to Google Sheets, generates a professionally formatted PDF invoice via an HTTP API, and delivers it to the client via Gmail — all within seconds.

The invoice includes the client's name, service description, amount due, payment method (GCash), and due date — branded with Christian V. Mercado's freelance identity.

How it works

1
Tally form submitted — Client details, service description, and amount entered via a Tally form.
2
Google Sheets logged — All form data is appended to a Google Sheet for record-keeping.
3
PDF generated via HTTP — Make.com sends the invoice data to an HTML-to-PDF API which returns a formatted invoice file.
4
PDF downloaded — The generated file is downloaded and prepared for email attachment.
5
Gmail sends invoice — A personalized email with the PDF attached is sent to the client instantly.

Key outcomes

Zero manual invoicing — form submission triggers the entire pipeline instantly
Professional PDF invoice auto-generated with client & payment details
Invoice delivered via Gmail within seconds of form submission
All invoices logged and traceable in Google Sheets

Tools used

Make.comTallyGoogle SheetsHTTP (PDF API)Gmail

Type

No-code automation

Category
